evidence.summary: Core session CRUD API. Handles GET/POST/DELETE/PATCH for sessions. Sub-resources: messages, git-info, rewind, turn-checkpoints, slash-commands, inspection, workspace (tree/file/diff), workflow (state/transition/start/report). Integrates workflowSessionStateService, workflowSessionCreateService, workflowSessionLinkService, workflowReportStore, workflowRuntimeService, workspaceService. Manages workflow transition queuing with enqueueWorkflowTransition. Has recent-projects and repository-context collection routes.

Central WebSocket connection handler (~2579 lines). Manages client and SDK WebSocket connections. Handles user_message (with workflow prompt assembly), permission_response, computer_use_permission_response, set_permission_mode, set_runtime_config, workflow_transition, prewarm_session, stop_generation, ping. Translates CLI messages (assistant/stream_event/user/control_request/result/system) to ServerMessage format. Manages session lifecycle with 30s cleanup timers, prewarm idle timeout, title generation, runtime config overrides, and workflow state ephemeral persistence. Key exports: handleWebSocket, sendToSession, closeSessionConnection, translateCliMessage, workflowNotificationForDesktop, updateSessionSlashCommands.

WebSocket event type definitions. ClientMessage types: prewarm_session, user_message, workflow_transition, permission_response, computer_use_permission_response, set_permission_mode, set_runtime_config, stop_generation, ping. ServerMessage types: connected, content_start, content_delta, tool_use_complete, tool_result, permission_request, computer_use_permission_request, message_complete, thinking, status, error, system_notification, pong, team_update, team_created, team_deleted, task_update, session_title_updated. Defines ChatState, TokenUsage, TeamMemberStatus, ComputerUsePermissionRequest/Response types.

Agents REST API with CRUD for agents (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/agents) and tasks API (GET /api/tasks, GET/POST /api/tasks/lists). Uses AgentService, taskService. Integrates with AgentTool for agent definitions with overrides. Exports handleAgentsApi.

Conversation API Routes for non-streaming chat operations. Routes: POST /api/sessions/:id/chat (enqueue message), GET /chat/status, POST /chat/stop. Maintains in-memory sessionStates Map (idle/thinking/tool_executing). Exports setSessionChatState, getSessionChatState for WebSocket integration.

Settings REST API. GET/PUT for user and project settings, GET/PUT for permission mode, GET for CLI launcher status. Uses SettingsService. Syncs alwaysThinkingEnabled to active sessions via conversationService.setMaxThinkingTokensForActiveSessions.

Models REST API. GET /api/models (available models from active provider or defaults), GET/PUT /api/models/current (current model with context tier support), GET/PUT /api/effort (effort levels: low/medium/high/max). Uses SettingsService, ProviderService. Supports composite model IDs like 'claude-opus-4-7:1m'.

Providers REST API. Full CRUD for providers (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE /api/providers). Sub-routes: presets, auth-status, settings (managed settings), official (activate official), test (test connectivity), activate. Uses ProviderService, CreateProviderSchema/UpdateProviderSchema/TestProviderSchema (zod). Records test failures to diagnosticsService.

Plugins REST API. GET /api/plugins (list), GET /api/plugins/detail (full data), POST /api/plugins/reload (with optional session reload), POST enable/disable/update/uninstall. Uses PluginService, conversationService for session plugin reload via requestControl.

Skills REST API. GET /api/skills (list all from user/project/plugin sources), GET /api/skills/detail (full tree + files). Collects skills from user dir, project dirs, and plugin directories. Exports listSkillSlashCommands used by sessions API. Uses loadAllPlugins, parseFrontmatter, getSkillDirCommands.

MCP (Model Context Protocol) REST API. Full CRUD for MCP servers (GET/POST/PUT/DELETE). Sub-routes: status, toggle, reconnect. Supports stdio/http/sse transports. Uses mcp config/auth/client modules. Includes host preflight checks, OAuth support, editable scopes (local/project/user). ~620 lines.
Search REST API. POST /api/search (global workspace search with cwd/maxResults/glob/caseSensitive options), POST /api/search/sessions (search session history). Uses SearchService.

Memory REST API. GET /api/memory/projects (list project-scoped memory dirs), GET /api/memory/files (list markdown files), GET/PUT /api/memory/file (read/update memory files). Manages memory files under ~/.claude/projects/<projectId>/memory/. Includes path traversal protection, project label resolution from session files and filesystem search.

Teams REST API. GET /api/teams (list), GET /api/teams/:name (detail), GET /:name/members/:id/transcript, POST /:name/members/:id/messages, POST /:name/members/:id/stop, DELETE /:name, DELETE /:name/members/:id. Uses teamService.

Diagnostics REST API. GET /api/diagnostics/status (log dir, retention), GET /api/diagnostics/events (recent events), POST /api/diagnostics/events (append client event), POST /api/diagnostics/export (tar.gz bundle), POST /api/diagnostics/open-log-dir, DELETE /api/diagnostics (clear). Uses diagnosticsService.

Computer Use API (~596 lines). GET /api/computer-use/status (Python3/venv/dependencies/permissions detection), POST /api/computer-use/setup (create venv, install deps), GET /api/computer-use/apps (list installed macOS/Windows apps), GET/PUT /api/computer-use/authorized-apps, POST /api/computer-use/open-settings, POST /api/computer-use/request-access. Manages venv lifecycle with marker-based interpreter tracking. Uses Tsinghua PyPI mirror for China.
Activity Stats REST API. GET /api/activity-stats (7d/30d/all ranges). Uses aggregateClaudeCodeStatsForRange from utils/stats.

Adapters REST API for IM adapter config. GET/PUT /api/adapters. Sub-routes: wechat (login/start, login/poll, unbind), dingtalk (registration/begin, registration/poll, unbind). Uses adapterService, WeChat protocol, DingTalk registration API.

Workflow Templates REST API (~817 lines). Full CRUD for workflow templates. Sub-routes: authoring, validate, duplicate, export, import/preview, import (commit). Uses WorkflowTemplateAuthoringService, WorkflowTemplateRegistryService, workflowPhaseSkillResolver. Supports builtin and user template sources. Handles import/export with dependency manifest and skill resolution diagnostics.

Status REST API. GET /api/status (health check with uptime/version), GET /api/status/diagnostics (node/bun version, platform, memory), GET /api/status/usage (token usage), GET /api/status/user (config dir, discovered projects). Tracks in-memory token usage via addUsage/resetUsage exports.
Doctor REST API. GET /api/doctor or GET /api/doctor/report (system health report), POST /api/doctor/repair (repair with optional targetIds). Uses DoctorService.

Filesystem browser & search API. GET /api/filesystem/browse (directory listing with search, fuzzy scoring), GET /api/filesystem/file (serve image files). Uses git ls-files and ripgrep for file discovery. Respects .gitignore. Path traversal protection via isAllowedFilesystemPath.

H5 Access REST API. GET/PUT /api/h5-access (settings), POST /api/h5-access/enable, POST /api/h5-access/disable, POST /api/h5-access/regenerate, POST /api/h5-access/verify (Bearer token validation). Uses H5AccessService.

Jiangxia OAuth REST API. POST /api/jiangxia-oauth/start (PKCE+state), GET /callback (token exchange), GET /api/jiangxia-oauth (login status), DELETE /api/jiangxia-oauth (logout). Uses jiangxiaOAuthService. Renders HTML callback pages.

Jiangxia OpenAI OAuth REST API. POST /api/jiangxia-openai-oauth/start (PKCE+state), GET /callback/openai (token exchange), GET /api/jiangxia-openai-oauth (login status with email/accountId), DELETE /api/jiangxia-openai-oauth (logout). Uses jiangxiaOpenAIOAuthService.
Desktop UI Preferences REST API. GET /api/desktop-ui/preferences, PUT /api/desktop-ui/preferences/sidebar. Uses DesktopUiPreferencesService.
Open Targets REST API. GET /api/open-targets (list targets), POST /api/open-targets/open (open target by ID+path), GET /api/open-targets/icons/:id (target icons). Uses openTargetService.
Scheduled Tasks REST API. GET/POST /api/scheduled-tasks, GET /api/scheduled-tasks/runs, GET /:id/runs, POST /:id/run (fire-and-forget execution), PUT/DELETE /:id. Uses CronService, cronScheduler.

Python runtime detection utility. Exports detectPythonRuntime that resolves Python interpreter from custom path, system python3/python/py, or venv. Platform-aware (win32 vs unix). Returns PythonRuntimeResolution with command, prefixArgs, source, version.
Adapter configuration service. Manages IM adapter config (serverUrl, telegram, feishu, wechat, dingtalk, pairing). Read/write config with sensitive field masking.
Agent CRUD service. Manages agent definitions with get/create/update/delete operations.

Core conversation service managing CLI subprocess lifecycle. Handles session start/stop, message sending, SDK connection management, permission mode, output callbacks. Key integration point between WebSocket handler and CLI subprocesses.
Session persistence service. Manages session JSONL files, session metadata, transcript reading, workDir resolution, session CRUD operations, file history snapshots, launch info.

Workflow runtime service (~1000+ lines). Handles workflow phase transitions, completion submissions (ready/blocked/unable), manual completion, prompt assembly, model resolution, start phase. Core state machine for workflow execution.
Workflow session state persistence. Read/write workflow state to disk with pointer-based storage. Handles state versioning and revision tracking.
Workflow template registry service. Lists templates from builtin and user sources, writes user templates, collects template skill catalog. Merges builtin and user workflow templates.

Workflow type definitions. Defines WorkflowSessionState, WorkflowSessionMetadata, WorkflowSessionSummary, WorkflowTransitionRequest, CompletionSubmission, WorkflowPhaseSkillSource, WorkflowModelResolution and related types.

Workflow tool policy service. Determines disallowed tools per workflow phase, provides prompt tool guidance, resolves scoped tool names. Used by WebSocket handler to apply tool restrictions during workflow execution.
Provider management service. CRUD for API providers, managed settings, auth status checking, provider testing (connectivity/proxy), official provider activation.
Settings service. Reads/writes user settings (~/.claude/settings.json), project settings, permission mode. Merges settings from multiple sources.
Team service. Manages multi-agent teams with member lifecycle (list, get, delete, send message, stop, get transcript). Coordinates multiple CLI subprocesses for team collaboration.
Task service. Manages background task lists and individual tasks with CRUD operations and task list reset.
Diagnostics service. Records diagnostic events, reads recent events, exports bundles, manages log directory, provides status info.
Search service. Provides workspace file search and session history search capabilities.
Plugin service. Lists plugins, gets plugin detail, enables/disables/updates/uninstalls plugins, reloads plugin set.
Workspace service. Provides workspace status, file tree reading, file content reading, and diff generation for session work directories.
Cron service. CRUD for scheduled cron tasks with cron expressions, prompts, and execution configuration.
Cron scheduler. Executes scheduled tasks, manages task runs, creates sessions for task execution. Fire-and-forget execution model.
Workflow final report builder. Constructs final report from workflow state when workflow completes.
Workflow report store. Persists and reads workflow final reports to/from disk.
Workflow session creation service. Resolves workflow templates, creates workflow session metadata, appends workflow metadata to sessions.
Workflow session link service. Creates linked workflow sessions from existing sessions, checking source session activity.
Workflow summary utilities. Converts workflow state to summary format and metadata format for API responses.
Workflow phase skill resolver. Resolves skill references in workflow phases against available skill catalog (user/project/plugin sources).
Workflow template authoring service. Executes authoring operations (guide, skill_catalog, skill_create, list, inspect, validate, create, update, duplicate, delete).
Workflow template authoring guide. Provides guidance documentation for creating workflow templates.
Workflow template validation. Validates and normalizes workflow templates, checks for builtin ID conflicts, duplicate template IDs, phase structure validity.
Title generation service. Derives quick placeholder titles from user messages, generates AI-powered titles, saves titles to session metadata.
Session rewind service. Provides session rewind functionality with preview and execute modes. Lists turn checkpoints and diffs.
Repository launch service. Resolves repository context for session creation, determines if worktree should be created.
Computer Use approval service. Manages approval requests for computer use permissions, resolves approvals, cancels sessions.
Notification service. Handles system notifications delivery.
Doctor service. Generates system health reports and performs repairs on detected issues.
H5 access service. Manages H5 web access settings, token generation/validation, enable/disable.
Jiangxia OAuth service. Manages OAuth PKCE flow, token storage/refresh, session lifecycle for Jiangxia platform authentication.
Jiangxia OpenAI OAuth service. Manages OAuth PKCE flow for OpenAI-compatible provider authentication via Jiangxia platform.
Managed settings service. Handles provider-managed settings that override user settings when a provider is active.
MCP host preflight. Inspects MCP host commands for prerequisites before server connection.
Open target service. Manages configurable open targets (IDEs, editors) for opening files externally.
Persistent storage migrations. Handles schema migrations for persistent storage formats.
Recoverable JSON file utility. Provides crash-safe JSON file read/write with recovery from corrupt files.
Desktop CLI launcher service. Ensures the desktop CLI launcher is installed and up to date.
Desktop UI preferences service. Reads/writes UI preferences including sidebar project preferences.
Team watcher. Monitors team member status changes and broadcasts updates via WebSocket.

Main HTTP+WebSocket server entry point. Uses Bun.serve() with configurable port/host. Handles routing for /ws/ (client WebSocket), /sdk/ (internal SDK WebSocket), /api/ (REST), /proxy/ (protocol-translating reverse proxy), /health, /callback (OAuth), and static H5 assets. Middleware chain: CORS resolution -> H5 access control -> auth enforcement -> route dispatch. Starts teamWatcher and cronScheduler on boot. Graceful shutdown kills all CLI subprocesses on SIGTERM/SIGINT.

API router mapping URL path segments to ~20 handler modules via switch statement. Resources: sessions, workflows, conversations, settings, models, permissions, scheduled-tasks, search, agents/tasks, status, teams, providers, jiangxia-oauth, jiangxia-openai-oauth, adapters, skills, mcp, plugins, computer-use, diagnostics, doctor, h5-access, activity-stats, open-targets, memory, desktop-ui, filesystem. Sub-routing for /api/sessions/:id/chat/* to conversations handler.

Authentication middleware. Two auth modes: (1) Anthropic API Key matching via Bearer token in Authorization header against ANTHROPIC_API_KEY env var; (2) H5 access token validation via H5AccessService. Exports: validateAuth(), validateRequestAuth() (tries API key then H5 token), requireAuth() (returns 401 Response on failure), requireH5Token() (H5-only validation).

CORS middleware for desktop and H5 access. Core function resolveCors() determines allowed/rejected state based on: (1) no origin = allowed, (2) H5 disabled or local origin = allowed, (3) isOriginAllowed callback for H5-enabled mode. Local origins include Tauri webview (tauri.localhost) and loopback hosts. Exports: corsHeaders(), resolveCors(), CorsResolution type.

Unified error handling. ApiError class extends Error with statusCode and code fields. Factory methods: badRequest(400), notFound(404), conflict(409), internal(500). errorResponse() function converts ApiError to JSON Response with status, or wraps unknown errors as 500 with diagnosticsService recording.

Protocol-translating reverse proxy handler. Receives Anthropic Messages API requests from CLI, transforms to OpenAI Chat Completions or Responses API format, forwards to upstream provider, transforms response back. Supports two routes: /proxy/v1/messages (active provider) and /proxy/providers/:providerId/v1/messages (explicit provider). Only handles POST. Rejects anthropic-format providers (no proxy needed). Supports streaming and non-streaming modes. Uses AbortSignal.timeout for upstream fetches (30s stream, 300s non-stream).

Type definitions for protocol transformation. Defines OpenAI types (OpenAIChatMessage, OpenAIChatRequest, OpenAIChatResponse, OpenAIChatStreamChunk, OpenAIResponsesRequest/Response/OutputItem) and Anthropic types (AnthropicContentBlock, AnthropicMessage, AnthropicRequest, AnthropicResponse). Content block types: text, image, tool_use, tool_result, thinking.

Request transformation: Anthropic Messages to OpenAI Chat Completions. Converts system prompts, user/assistant messages, tool calls, tool results. Filters out BatchTool. Maps thinking budget_tokens to reasoning_effort (low/medium/high). Omits max_tokens to let upstream use defaults. Converts stop_sequences to stop.

Response transformation: OpenAI Chat Completions to Anthropic Messages. Handles multiple reasoning formats: reasoning_content (DeepSeek, OpenRouter, XAI, Perplexity), reasoning (GLM-5, Cerebras, Groq), thinking_blocks (OpenAI o-series). Maps finish_reason: stop->end_turn, tool_calls->tool_use, length->max_tokens. Converts tool_calls to tool_use blocks. Maps usage including cached_tokens.

SSE streaming transformation: OpenAI Chat Completions to Anthropic Messages format. Implements state machine tracking content blocks (text, thinking, tool_use). Handles block transitions with priority: tool_calls > text > reasoning. Supports parallel tool call streaming via Map<number, ToolBlockState>. Follows LiteLLM patterns for correctness. Holds message_delta until usage chunk arrives. Provider-specific reasoning format handling (reasoning_content, reasoning, thinking_blocks).

Request transformation: Anthropic Messages to OpenAI Responses API. Converts messages to input items (message, function_call, function_call_output). Maps system to instructions. Maps thinking budget to reasoning.effort. Filters BatchTool from tools.

Response transformation: OpenAI Responses API to Anthropic Messages. Converts output items: message (text, refusal), function_call (tool_use), reasoning (thinking). Maps status: completed->end_turn/tool_use, failed->end_turn, incomplete->max_tokens.

SSE streaming transformation: OpenAI Responses API to Anthropic Messages format. Handles events: response.created, response.output_item.added (function_call), response.content_part.added, response.output_text.delta, response.refusal.delta, response.function_call_arguments.delta, response.output_text.done, response.function_call_arguments.done, response.completed. Uses indexByKey and toolIndexByItemId maps for content block tracking.

Tool argument parsing utilities. parseOpenAIToolArguments(): handles null, string (JSON.parse with fallback to {raw: value}), object passthrough. stringifyOpenAIToolArguments(): returns empty string for null, passthrough for string, JSON.stringify for objects.

Provider preset configuration. Loads providerPresets.json and validates with Zod schemas. ProviderPresetSchema: id, name, baseUrl, apiFormat (anthropic/openai_chat/openai_responses), defaultModels (main/haiku/sonnet/opus), needsApiKey, websiteUrl, apiKeyUrl, promoText, featured, authStrategy, defaultEnv, modelContextWindows. Exports PROVIDER_PRESETS validated array.

Provider type definitions with Zod schemas. ApiFormat: anthropic/openai_chat/openai_responses. ProviderAuthStrategy: api_key/auth_token/auth_token_empty_api_key/dual_same_token/dual_dummy. SavedProvider: id, presetId, name, apiKey, authStrategy, baseUrl, apiFormat, models, autoCompactWindow, modelContextWindows, notes. ProvidersIndex: schemaVersion, activeId, providers[]. CRUD schemas: CreateProviderSchema, UpdateProviderSchema, TestProviderSchema. ProviderTestResult with connectivity and proxy steps.

Core server types. ServerConfig: port, host, authToken, unix, idleTimeoutMs, maxSessions, workspace. SessionState: starting/running/detached/stopping/stopped. SessionInfo: id, status, createdAt, workDir, process, sessionKey. SessionIndexEntry: sessionId, transcriptSessionId, cwd, permissionMode, createdAt, lastActiveAt. SessionIndex: Record<string, SessionIndexEntry>. connectResponseSchema: session_id, ws_url, work_dir.

H5 (mobile browser) access policy. Request classification: local-trusted (loopback + local origin), internal-sdk (loopback + /sdk/ path), h5-browser (everything else). isLoopbackHost() handles IPv4/IPv6/mapped addresses. shouldRequireH5Token(): requires token for h5-browser requests to /api/, /proxy/, /ws/ paths when H5 enabled. shouldBlockDisabledH5Access(): blocks h5-browser from /api/, /proxy/, /ws/, /sdk/ when H5 disabled and no explicit auth.

Static file serving for H5 (mobile web) shell. Resolves dist directory from multiple candidates (env vars, app root, cwd). Path traversal protection via containedPath() checking relative path stays within root. MIME type detection for common web formats. Cache-Control: immutable for /assets/, no-store for other files. Supports GET and HEAD methods.

WebSocket-based direct connect session manager. DirectConnectSessionManager class manages WebSocket connection to server. Handles SDK messages (assistant, result, system), control requests (permission: can_use_tool), and forwards to callbacks. Methods: connect(), sendMessage(), respondToPermissionRequest(), sendInterrupt(), disconnect(), isConnected(). Sends SDKUserMessage format matching --input-format stream-json.

Creates sessions on direct-connect servers via REST POST /sessions. Validates response with connectResponseSchema (Zod). Returns DirectConnectConfig (serverUrl, sessionId, wsUrl, authToken) and optional workDir. Throws DirectConnectError on failures.

Auto-generated build stub. Proxy-based no-op module for ant-internal feature() gated modules. Exports __stubMissing flag and named stubs (createCachedMCState, isCachedMicrocompactEnabled, etc.). Used as bun build resolver placeholder.
Auto-generated build stub identical to index.ts pattern. Proxy-based no-op for ant-internal feature() gated modules.

Provider presets JSON data. Contains preset entries for: official (Claude, anthropic format), deepseek (anthropic format, auth_token strategy, 1M context), zhipuglm (anthropic format). Each has id, name, baseUrl, apiFormat, defaultModels, needsApiKey, websiteUrl, optional authStrategy/defaultEnv/modelContextWindows.
MIT License attribution for cc-switch by Jason Young. Protocol transformation logic in proxy/ directory is derived from https://github.com/farion1231/cc-switch.

Tests for proxy streaming SSE transformation. Uses bun:test. Helper functions: makeStream() creates ReadableStream from chunks, collectSse() parses SSE events. Tests openaiChatStreamToAnthropic with basic text streaming scenarios.

Tests for proxy protocol transformation. Tests anthropicToOpenaiChat (text messages, system prompts, stop_sequences), anthropicToOpenaiResponses, openaiChatToAnthropic, openaiResponsesToAnthropic. Uses bun:test with AnthropicRequest/Response typed test data.

Tests for SessionService and Sessions API. Uses bun:test with temp directory setup for config isolation. Imports sessionService, conversationService, repositoryLaunchService. Tests workflow session creation with WORKFLOW_ERROR_CODES. Uses beforeEach/afterEach for setup/teardown with CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR env var.

Tests for H5 access policy. Tests isLoopbackHost (localhost, 127.0.0.1, ::1, non-loopback). Tests classifyH5Request for Tauri WebView, internal SDK, adapter API, spoofed loopback. Tests shouldRequireH5Token and shouldBlockDisabledH5Access with local/remote contexts.

Tests for WebSocket handler. Uses bun:test with mock ServerWebSocket. Tests handleWebSocket, getActiveSessionIds, closeSessionConnection. Imports conversationService, computerUseApprovalService, sessionService, WorkflowSessionStateService. Helper: makeClientSocket() creates mock WS with sent[] tracking.

Tests for ProviderService and Providers REST API. Uses bun:test with temp directory for config isolation. Tests handleProvidersApi with mock Request objects. Helper: makeRequest() creates Request+URL+segments for API handler testing.

Mock SDK CLI fixture for testing. Emulates Claude CLI WebSocket SDK behavior. Accepts --sdk-url, --session-id args. Configurable via env vars: MOCK_SDK_INIT_MODE, MOCK_SDK_INIT_DELAY_MS, MOCK_SDK_STREAM_DELAY_MS, MOCK_SDK_EXIT_AFTER_OPEN_MS, etc. Emits JSON payloads over WebSocket.
Tests for CORS middleware. Tests corsHeaders with localhost, Tauri WebView, arbitrary origins. Tests resolveCors with H5 token mode active/inactive, blocked/configured origins.

AgentTool is the primary delegation tool. Uses buildTool() with Zod schemas (baseInputSchema + fullInputSchema). Supports subagent_type selection, model override (sonnet/opus/haiku), run_in_background, worktree isolation, remote isolation (ant-only), multi-agent spawning (team_name + name), and fork subagent experiment. Depends on runAgent, loadAgentsDir, forkSubagent, agentToolUtils, LocalAgentTask, RemoteAgentTask. Has complex permission filtering via filterDeniedAgents. Output is union of completed/async_launched/teammate_spawned/remote_launched statuses.

BashTool executes shell commands. Uses buildTool() with strict mode. Input: command, timeout, description, run_in_background, dangerouslyDisableSandbox. Extensive security: bashPermissions, bashSecurity, readOnlyValidation, pathValidation, modeValidation, sedValidation. Supports sandbox mode (shouldUseSandbox), sed edit parsing, auto-backgrounding, progress reporting. Output includes stdout, stderr, interrupted flag, backgroundTaskId. Shared utilities with PowerShellTool via BashTool/utils.ts. Tracks git operations via shared/gitOperationTracking.ts.

FileEditTool performs string-replace edits on files. Input: file_path, old_string, new_string, replace_all. Validates: file existence, read-before-write, staleness check (mtime vs readFileState), secret detection (checkTeamMemSecrets), settings file validation, UNC path safety. Uses findActualString for quote normalization. Notifies LSP servers (didChange/didSave), VSCode, and file history system. Depends on diagnosticTracker, getLspServerManager, notifyVscodeFileUpdated. Max file size: 1 GiB.

FileWriteTool creates or overwrites files. Input: file_path (absolute), content. Validates: read-before-write, staleness, secret detection, UNC path safety. Shares pattern with FileEditTool: LSP notification, VSCode notification, file history tracking, diagnostic tracker. Preserves encoding, always writes LF line endings. Returns create/update type with structuredPatch.

SkillTool invokes slash-command skills. Input: skill (name), args (optional). Two execution modes: inline (injects messages into conversation) and forked (runs in isolated sub-agent via runAgent). Supports remote canonical skills (ant-only experimental). Permission system: deny/allow rules, auto-allow for safe properties. Depends on processPromptSlashCommand, prepareForkedCommandContext, runAgent. Records skill usage for ranking. Supports model/effort override per skill.

WorkflowTool is a feature-gated stub (ant-internal). The file exports a Proxy-based no-op stub with __stubMissing=true. Real implementation is behind feature() gate. The workflow directory contains WorkflowPermissionRequest.ts, constants.ts, createWorkflowCommand.ts, and bundled/index.ts for bundled workflow support.

TaskCreateTool creates tasks in the task list (TodoV2). Input: subject, description, activeForm, metadata. Uses createTask() from utils/tasks.ts. Executes task-created hooks (executeTaskCreatedHooks) which can block task creation. Auto-expands task list view. isEnabled gated on isTodoV2Enabled(). shouldDefer=true, isConcurrencySafe=true.

SendMessageTool is the multi-agent swarm messaging protocol. Input: to (recipient or *), summary, message (string or structured: shutdown_request, shutdown_response, plan_approval_response). Supports: mailbox-based team messaging, broadcast, in-process subagent routing (queuePendingMessage), agent resume from disk transcript, UDS cross-session, bridge (Remote Control). isEnabled gated on isAgentSwarmsEnabled(). Complex validation: structured messages can't be broadcast, shutdown_response must go to team-lead, bridge messages require active connection.

WebFetchTool fetches URL content and processes with a prompt. Input: url (validated), prompt. Read-only, concurrency-safe. Uses preapproved host list for auto-allow. Applies prompt to markdown via Haiku for non-preapproved URLs. Handles redirects, binary content (PDFs saved to disk). Permission system: domain-based rules. Max markdown length enforced. shouldDefer=true.

WebSearchTool performs web searches. Input: query, allowed_domains, blocked_domains. Supports multiple backends: Anthropic native (web_search_20250305), Tavily, Brave. Auto-fallback from native to external providers. Progress reporting for query updates and result counts. isEnabled gated on isWebSearchEnabledForModel(). Read-only, concurrency-safe. Output: query, results (mixed text+search hits), durationSeconds.

GlobTool finds files by pattern. Input: pattern, path (optional, defaults to cwd). Read-only, concurrency-safe. Uses custom glob utility. Results limited to 100 files (globLimits.maxResults). Returns relative paths to save tokens. Validates path existence and is-directory check. UNC path safety check.

GrepTool searches file contents via ripgrep. Input: pattern, path, glob, output_mode (content/files_with_matches/count), context flags (-B/-A/-C), -n, -i, type, head_limit (default 250), offset, multiline. Read-only, concurrency-safe. Excludes VCS dirs automatically. Sorts results by mtime for files_with_matches mode. Applies ignore patterns from permission context. Max 500 char line length.

TodoWriteTool manages session task checklists (legacy TodoV1). Input: todos (TodoListSchema). isEnabled when TodoV2 is disabled (inverse of TaskCreateTool). Stores todos in appState keyed by agentId or sessionId. Structural nudge: if 3+ tasks completed without verification step, prompts to spawn verification agent. shouldDefer=true.

MCPTool is a template/stub for MCP (Model Context Protocol) tool integration. Input: passthrough (any object). All methods (name, description, prompt, call) are overridden by mcpClient.ts at runtime. Uses passthrough permission (requires user approval). isMcp=true flag. Real schemas come from MCP server definitions.

Shared utility for detecting git operations in shell command output. Detects: git commit (with amend/cherry-pick), git push, git merge, git rebase, gh pr create/edit/merge/comment/close/ready, glab mr create, curl-based PR creation (Bitbucket/GitHub/GitLab APIs). Tracks via OTLP counters and analytics events. Auto-links session to PR on creation. Used by both BashTool and PowerShellTool.

Shared tool utilities: tagMessagesWithToolUseID (tags user messages with source tool use ID for transient message handling), getToolUseIDFromParentMessage (extracts tool_use block ID from assistant message). Used by SkillTool for message tagging.

Shared module for teammate creation, extracted from TeammateTool for reuse by AgentTool. Handles tmux/in-process backend detection, team file management, environment variable inheritance, teammate color assignment, and spawn configuration. Depends on swarm utilities (backends/registry, teamHelpers, spawnInProcess, spawnUtils).

Core agent execution loop. Runs a sub-agent with given definition, messages, tools, and model. Handles streaming, tool use dispatch, and message normalization. Central to AgentTool and SkillTool forked execution.

Fork subagent experiment. Enables forking parent agent context (cache-identical API prefixes) instead of using dedicated agent definitions. Builds forked messages from parent's full assistant message. Gate-controlled via isForkSubagentEnabled().

Loads agent definitions from .claude/agents/ directory. Handles built-in agents, custom agents from filesystem, MCP server requirements filtering. AgentDefinition type includes agentType, model, color, isolation, background, requiredMcpServers, allowedTools.

Bash command permission system. Checks permission rules against commands, handles wildcard patterns, extracts prefixes for matching. Used by BashTool.checkPermissions and preparePermissionMatcher. Includes commandHasAnyCd for compound commands.
Bash security validation. Parses commands for security concerns, validates against sandbox restrictions. Integrates with SandboxManager for sandboxed execution mode.

PowerShellTool is the Windows equivalent of BashTool. Nearly identical architecture: buildTool(), Zod input schema (command, timeout, description, run_in_background, dangerouslyDisableSandbox), sandbox support, progress reporting, git operation tracking. Has own permission system (powershellPermissions), security (powershellSecurity), validation modules. Shares BashTool/utils.ts for image handling and output processing.

FileReadTool reads files from the filesystem. Handles text files (with line numbers, offset/limit), images (compression, resize), PDFs (page extraction), Jupyter notebooks (cell mapping). Depends on imageResizer, pdf utils, notebook utils. Supports pages parameter for PDFs. Permission system via checkReadPermissionForTool.

LSPTool provides Language Server Protocol operations. Supports: go_to_definition, find_references, hover, document_symbols, workspace_symbols, prepare_call_hierarchy, incoming_calls, outgoing_calls. Uses getLspServerManager for LSP server communication. Has file size limit (10MB). Falls back to ctags when LSP unavailable.
Root React component. Renders AppShell, initializes desktop notification navigation, and uses useScheduledTaskDesktopNotifications hook.

Core HTTP API client. Manages base URL (default http://127.0.0.1:3456), auth token, request timeout (30s default), diagnostic event reporting. Exports api object with get/post/put/patch/delete methods.
API client for IM adapter management (WeChat, DingTalk). Supports config CRUD, WeChat QR login flow, DingTalk device code registration flow.
API client for agent definitions. Lists agents with optional cwd filter. Types define AgentSource (built-in, plugin, userSettings, etc.) and AgentDefinition.
API client for user activity statistics. Supports 7d/30d/all ranges. Returns sessions, messages, streaks, daily activity, token usage by model, peak activity data.
API client for CLI task management. Lists task lists, gets tasks per list, gets single task, resets completed task lists.
API client for computer use feature. Manages Python environment setup, accessibility/screen recording permissions, authorized apps whitelist, and grant flags (clipboard, system key combos).
API client for desktop UI preferences. Manages sidebar project ordering, pinning, hiding, and organization mode.
Tests for API client: auth token handling, diagnostic event reporting, token redaction in diagnostics, recursive diagnostics prevention, URL resolution.

Comprehensive page rendering tests. Covers EmptySession (slash picker, mascot, composer, plus menu, draft context), ActiveSession (chat, MCP panel, skills panel, /help, /status, /plugin, context usage indicator), AgentTeams, ScheduledTasks, ToolInspection, Sidebar. Tests mock skillsApi, mcpApi, sessionsApi, providersApi. Reveals store dependencies: useUIStore, useChatStore, useSettingsStore, useSessionStore, useProviderStore, useSessionRuntimeStore, useTabStore.
Tests for Settings page. Reveals dependencies on useSettingsStore, useUIStore, useUpdateStore, provider types, Tauri invoke/dialog/process APIs.
Tests for McpSettings page. Reveals useMcpStore with servers list, CRUD operations, toggle/reconnect, and useSessionStore dependency.

Unified sidecar entry point for server/cli/adapters modes. Consolidates multiple bun binaries into one to save ~100MB+ runtime. Server mode starts HTTP server, CLI mode runs CLI entrypoint, adapters mode starts IM adapters (Feishu, Telegram, WeChat, DingTalk) with credential gating.

Sidecar mode resolution and argument parsing. Resolves server/cli/adapters mode from args or executable name. Supports desktop CLI names: claude-jiangxia, cc-jiangxia, claude-haha. Parses --app-root flag.

Core Tauri Rust backend (~2270 lines). Manages: server sidecar lifecycle (spawn/wait/kill), adapter sidecar lifecycle (per-IM platform processes), terminal PTY sessions (spawn/write/resize/kill with UTF-8 decoding), window state persistence (save/restore with multi-monitor validation), system tray, macOS native notifications (via C FFI to macos_notifications.m), app mode (default/portable), terminal shell resolution (Windows pwsh/cmd/custom), zoom control. Tauri commands exposed: get_server_url, restart_adapters_sidecar, prepare_for_update_install, terminal_spawn/write/resize/kill, macos_notification_*, get/set_app_mode, detect_portable_dir, set_app_zoom.

Tauri entry point. Handles portable mode startup detection (env var > persisted app-mode.json > auto-detect data files), WebView2 user data folder redirection for portable installs, then delegates to lib::run().
Rust crate config. Dependencies: tauri 2.10.3 (tray-icon), portable-pty 0.9.0, serde/serde_json, tauri plugins (shell, dialog, process, updater, notification, single-instance), reqwest 0.13.

Tauri app config. Product: 'Claude Code Jiangxia' v0.2.8. Window: 1440x960, min 960x640. CSP configured. Bundles claude-sidecar as externalBin. Updater configured with GitHub releases endpoint. NSIS installer hooks for Windows.

Build script for sidecar binaries. Uses Bun.build with compile target. Detects host triple via rustc. Maps Rust targets to Bun targets. Supports macOS ad-hoc code signing. Marks optional npm packages as external (OpenTelemetry, AWS SDK, sharp, etc.).
Desktop package config. React 18.3, Vite 8, Vitest 3, TailwindCSS 4, Zustand 5, xterm 6, marked 15, mermaid 11, shiki 4, lucide-react, react-diff-viewer-continued. Tauri plugins as optional deps.

Primary session management API. Exports sessionsApi with 30+ methods: list, create, delete, batchDelete, rename, getMessages, getGitInfo, getInspection, getWorkspaceStatus/Tree/File/Diff, getTurnCheckpoints, rewind, workflow CRUD (templates, transitions, reports, linked sessions), getRecentProjects, getRepositoryContext, getSlashCommands. Rich type exports for SessionListItem, SessionGitInfo, RepositoryContextResult, SessionRewindResponse, WorkflowTemplate*, SessionUsageSnapshot, SessionContextSnapshot, SessionInspectionResponse, WorkspaceChangedFile.

WebSocket connection manager. Singleton WebSocketManager class with per-session connection pool. Supports connect/disconnect/disconnectAll/send/onMessage/clearHandlers. Auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (max 30s). Ping loop every 30s. Pending message queue. Exports wsManager singleton and buildSessionWebSocketUrl helper.
MCP (Model Context Protocol) server management API. CRUD operations for MCP servers: list, status, create, update, remove, toggle, reconnect. All methods accept optional cwd parameter.
Model management API. list, getCurrent, setCurrent, getEffort, setEffort. Uses ModelInfo and EffortLevel types.
Skills catalog API. list (with cwd), catalog (catalogOnly mode), detail (by source+name). 120s timeout. Uses SkillCatalogItem, SkillDetail types.
User settings and permissions API. getUser, updateUser, getPermissionMode, setPermissionMode, getCliLauncherStatus. Exports CliLauncherStatus type.
Third-party provider management API. CRUD for providers, presets, authStatus, getSettings/updateSettings, activate/activateOfficial, test/testConfig. Supports multiple provider backends.
Plugin lifecycle API. list, detail, enable, disable, update, uninstall, reload. Supports scope and sessionId parameters. 120s timeout on reload.
Scheduled tasks (cron) API. list, create, update, delete, runTask, getRecentRuns, getTaskRuns.
Team management API. list, get, getMemberTranscript, sendMemberMessage, delete, deleteMember, stopMember, deleteTeam.
Tauri terminal integration API. spawn, write, resize, kill, onOutput, onExit, getBashPath, setBashPath. Only available in Tauri runtime.
Diagnostics and logging API. getStatus, getEvents, recordEvent, exportBundle, openLogDir, clear.
Doctor/repair API for checking data integrity. report, repair, reportAndRepair. Validates JSON/JSONL files.
Filesystem browsing API. browse (directory listing with optional files), search (with maxResults=200).
H5 remote access management API. get, enable, disable, regenerate, update (allowedOrigins, publicBaseUrl).
Jiangxia OAuth authentication API. start (returns authorizeUrl+state), status (loggedIn, expiresAt, scopes, subscriptionType), logout.
Memory file management API. listProjects, listFiles, readFile, saveFile.
External editor/file-manager open targets API. list (cached with TTL), open. Normalizes icon URLs.
Code and session search API. search (POST with query/cwd/maxResults/glob), searchSessions.

Main chat composer component (~1600 lines). Handles message input, file attachments (drag-drop, paste, picker), slash commands (/mcp, /skills, etc.), @ file references, workflow template selection, linked workflow context strategy, repository launch controls, workspace references. Integrates with 8+ stores (chatStore, tabStore, uiStore, sessionStore, sessionRuntimeStore, teamStore, settingsStore, workspaceChatContextStore). Supports hero/default/compact variants. Mobile-responsive with Tauri native file picker.

Chat message list renderer (~1228 lines). Renders UIMessage[] as a virtualized scrollable list. Supports message types: user_text, assistant_text, thinking, tool_use, tool_result, permission_request, error, task_summary, memory_event, goal_event, background_task, system. Features: auto-scroll with jump-to-latest, turn change cards with undo/rewind, tool call grouping, chat selection menu for adding references, session scroll snapshots. Exports buildRenderModel, getCompletedTurnTargets, getLatestCompletedTurnTarget, MessageBlock.

Root application shell. Manages bootstrap sequence (initializeDesktopServerUrl, fetchSettings), sidebar state, mobile viewport detection, H5 connection errors, startup errors. Renders Sidebar + ContentRouter + TabBar + ToastContainer + UpdateChecker.

Session sidebar with project grouping, search, batch mode, context menus, pinning, project organization (by project/recent/time), sort options. Uses localStorage for project order/pins/hidden/organization preferences. Auto-refresh every 30s.
Content routing component. Routes active tab to EmptySession, ActiveSession, ScheduledTasks, Settings, or TerminalSettings based on tab type. Terminal tabs rendered with visibility toggling.
Reusable button component. Variants: primary (gradient), secondary (bordered), danger, ghost. Sizes: sm/md/lg. Loading spinner support.
Portal-based modal dialog. Backdrop overlay, ESC to close, configurable width, title, footer slot.
Form input with label, error state, required indicator. Auto-generates id from label.
Form textarea with label, error state, required indicator. Min-height 120px, resizable.
Generic dropdown selector with typed values. Supports icons, descriptions, selected state, keyboard ESC dismiss.
Toast notification system. Types: success, error, warning, info. Fixed bottom-right positioning. Auto-dismiss via store.
SVG loading spinner with configurable size.
Confirmation dialog built on Modal. Supports primary/danger confirm variants, loading state.
Clipboard copy button with 1.5s copied feedback. Uses copyTextToClipboard utility.
Mobile-optimized bottom sheet dialog. Portal-based, ESC dismiss, header/footer slots.
Directory picker with recent projects and filesystem browse modes. Module-level cache (30s TTL). Supports Tauri runtime detection.
Displays current project context (repo name, branch, worktree info) as a chip/badge.
Repository launch configuration: directory picker, branch selector, worktree toggle. Fetches repository context from API.
Desktop app update checker. Shows download progress, release notes (rendered as markdown), install prompt. Tauri-only.
Assistant message bubble. Renders markdown content, inline image gallery, message action bar. Supports document layout for code-heavy content.
User message bubble with attachments gallery and copy action bar.
Individual tool call display. Shows tool icon, summary, expandable details/preview. Supports tool types: Bash, Read, Write, Edit, Glob, Grep, Agent, WebSearch, WebFetch, NotebookEdit, Skill.
Groups consecutive tool calls with collapsible summary. Memory tool activity tracking. Agent lifecycle support.
Collapsible thinking/reasoning display. Shows preview line, expandable full content with auto-scroll. Active state with animated dots.
Streaming status indicator. Shows verb (Thinking/Running/Working), elapsed time, token usage. Translates server verbs.
Tool permission request dialog. Shows tool details, approve/deny buttons, diff preview for Edit/Write. Uses DOMPurify for sanitization.
Syntax-highlighted code viewer using react-shiki. Custom warm-code TextMate theme. Line numbers, copy button, maxLines support.
Diff viewer using react-diff-viewer-continued with prism-react-renderer. Custom warm syntax theme. Language inference from file extension.
Mermaid diagram renderer with zoom/pan, modal fullview, SVG export, DOMPurify sanitization.
Task progress summary display. Shows completion count, individual task status icons.
Turn change card showing file changes per turn. Expandable diff preview. Undo action support.

Slash command utilities. Defines PANEL_SLASH_COMMANDS (mcp, skills, help, status, cost, context), SETTINGS_SLASH_COMMANDS (plugin, memory, doctor), FALLBACK_SLASH_COMMANDS (20+ commands). Functions: filterSlashCommands, findSlashTrigger, mergeSlashCommands, replaceSlashToken, resolveSlashUiAction.
Clipboard utility. Async clipboard API with fallback to document.execCommand('copy').
File drop hook for composer. Supports both browser drag-drop and Tauri native drag-drop events. Validates drop position within panel bounds.
Model selection dropdown. Shows providers with their models, effort level control, runtime selection per session. Portal-based positioning.
Permission mode selector (default, acceptEdits, plan, bypassPermissions, dontAsk). Confirm dialog for dangerous modes. Uses DOMPurify for description sanitization.
Tab bar with drag-reorder, close confirmation for running sessions, new session button, terminal tabs, settings/scheduled tabs, OpenProjectMenu integration.
Status bar showing project name and current model label.
Windows-only Tauri window controls (minimize/maximize/close). Detects platform and Tauri runtime.
Title bar with logo, navigation arrows, view tabs, window controls. Tauri drag region support.
React error boundary with fallback UI. Reports errors via diagnosticsCapture. Shows DoctorPanel on crash.
Doctor diagnostics panel. Shows report items, repair actions. Used in error boundary fallback.
Plugin list view with enable/disable/update/uninstall actions.
Plugin detail view with configuration and actions.
Skills catalog browser.
Skill detail view with source and name parameters.
Day-of-week picker for scheduled task cron configuration.
Claude official login/OAuth flow component.
H5 remote connection view for mobile browser access.
Project opening menu with recent projects and directory picker.
Startup error display with retry and diagnostics.
Markdown rendering component with code highlighting, variant support (default/document).
Interactive question prompt from assistant. Renders question with input options.
Attachment display gallery for images and files in composer/message.
Visual overlay shown during file drag-drop on composer.
Modal for computer use permission requests.
Context window usage indicator showing token consumption.
@ file reference search menu with filesystem browsing.
Full-screen image gallery modal.
Inline image gallery extracted from markdown content.
Panel for slash command execution (mcp, skills, help, status, cost, context).
Message action bar with copy button. Positioned at start/end of messages.
Session task bar showing active tasks and status.
Terminal chrome wrapper for embedded terminal display.
Tool result display block. Shows content with error styling.

Application bootstrap entry point. Uses React 18 createRoot, lazy-loads App/ErrorBoundary/diagnosticsCapture/uiStore. Initializes theme and app zoom before render. Declares global Window augmentation for __CC_JIANGXIA_BOOTSTRAPPED__ and __CC_JIANGXIA_SHOW_STARTUP_ERROR__. Runs persistence migrations on module load.

Custom i18n system (no external library). Supports en/zh locales. Exports translate() function, useTranslation() React hook, and standalone t() function. Uses Zustand settingsStore for locale state. Supports interpolation params via {key} placeholders.

English translation catalog with 1700+ keys organized by domain: common, sidebar, titlebar, workspace, status, settings (providers/permissions/activity/terminal/diagnostics/workflows/mcp/agents/skills/memory/plugins/about/computerUse/general), empty session, chat, streaming, permission, computerUseApproval, question, thinking, tool, tasks, cron, teams, scheduled pages, updates, sessions, app shell, error codes, tabs.

Chinese translation catalog. Type-constrained by Record<TranslationKey, string> from en.ts. Complete 1:1 mapping with English catalog. All UI strings localized including settings, workflows, adapters (WeChat/DingTalk/Telegram/Feishu), MCP, plugins, skills, agents, teams, scheduled tasks.

Core chat state management. Zustand store managing per-session chat state: messages, streaming, tool execution, permissions, background agents, goals, composer drafts. Handles WebSocket message routing (content_delta, tool_use, permission_request, message_complete, error, system_notification). Supports workflow transitions, team member messaging, optimistic session titles. Uses 50ms streaming throttle for content_delta. Immutable state updates via updateSessionIn helper.

Session list management. Zustand store for CRUD operations on sessions: fetch, create, delete (single/batch), rename, batch selection mode. Supports workflow session creation. Optimistic updates with server sync. Deduplicates sessions by ID preserving most recently modified. Syncs tab titles on fetch.

Application settings management. Zustand store for: permission mode, model selection, effort level, thinking mode, locale (en/zh), theme (light/dark/white), web fetch preflight, desktop notifications, terminal shell config, web search settings, update proxy, H5 access, response language, UI zoom, app mode (system/portable). Fetches all settings in parallel on init. Persisted locale via localStorage key cc-jiangxia-locale.

Whimsical loading spinner verb list (~190 verbs). Exports randomSpinnerVerb() that returns a random verb for chat loading state. Verbs range from serious (Calculating, Architecting) to playful (Booping, Moonwalking, Shenaniganing).
Official Anthropic model catalog. Defines OFFICIAL_DEFAULT_MODEL_ID as 'claude-opus-4-7'. Lists 3 official models: Opus 4.7 (1m context), Sonnet 4.6 (200k), Haiku 4.5 (200k).

IM adapter configuration store. Manages Telegram/Feishu/WeChat/DingTalk adapter configs. Generates pairing codes with 6-char alphanumeric, 60min TTL. Supports WeChat QR login flow, DingTalk QR registration. Triggers Tauri sidecar restart on config changes.

API provider management store. Manages SavedProvider CRUD, presets, activation, testing. On provider update, refreshes connected sessions that use the updated provider. Supports official Anthropic provider activation without API key.

Tab management store. Manages open tabs (session/settings/scheduled/terminal types). Persists tabs to localStorage (cc-jiangxia-open-tabs). Supports terminal tab creation with auto-numbering, tab reordering, session ID replacement. Defines special tab IDs: SETTINGS_TAB_ID, SCHEDULED_TAB_ID, TERMINAL_TAB_PREFIX.

Agent team management store. Manages team CRUD, member lifecycle (join/leave/stop), member transcript polling (1.5s interval). Generates synthetic session IDs for member tabs (team-member:{agentId}). Validates member names against CLI flags and API methods. Supports member color assignment from AGENT_COLORS palette.

Skill browser store. Manages skill list, catalog, and detail view. Fetches skills from skillsApi with optional cwd scoping. Supports return tab tracking (skills vs plugins) for navigation breadcrumbs.

MCP server management store. Manages server list with deduplication by scope+name+projectPath. Supports CRUD, toggle, reconnect, status refresh. Project-scoped servers (local/project) attach project path for context isolation.

Desktop runtime detection and H5 connection utilities. Detects Tauri vs browser H5 runtime. Manages H5 connection state (serverUrl + token) via localStorage with legacy key migration (cc-haha-* -> cc-jiangxia-*). Exports H5ConnectionRequiredError class with failure reasons.

Desktop notification system. Manages native OS notifications with deduplication, cooldown scopes, and attention requests. Supports Tauri native notifications and browser Notification API fallback. Platform-aware settings URL detection (macOS/Windows). Tracks notification targets for click-to-navigate.

LocalStorage migration system. Handles schema versioning (current v1) and legacy key migration from cc-haha-* to cc-jiangxia-* namespace. Migrates tabs, session runtime, theme, locale, H5 connection, and app zoom keys.

Application zoom control system. Manages CSS zoom level (0.5x-2x range, 0.1 step, 0.01 control step). Supports keyboard shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl +/-/0). Persists to localStorage. Applies via CSS custom property --app-zoom and body zoom style. Handles legacy key migration.
Barrel export file for workflow components. Re-exports WorkflowTemplatePicker, WorkflowStatusPanel, WorkflowReportLink, WorkflowTransitionControls and their type exports.

Task list component. Renders stat cards (total/active/disabled) and accordion task rows. Uses useTranslation() hook for i18n. StatCard uses CSS custom properties for theming (--color-surface-info, --color-text-primary, --color-text-secondary, --radius-lg).

Main active session page. Orchestrates chat input, message list, workspace panel, team status bar, terminal panel, workflow controls. Uses 12+ Zustand stores. Supports workspace resizing, terminal panel toggling, goal tracking strip, background agent display. CSS custom properties for layout (--color-border, --color-surface).

Global keyboard shortcuts hook. Handles: Cmd+N (new session), Cmd+K (focus sidebar search), Escape (close modal), Cmd+. (stop generation), zoom shortcuts (Cmd/Ctrl +/-/0). Uses refs for stale closure prevention.

Open target store for launching files in external editors. Manages target list with 60s client cache TTL. Supports primary target selection with fallback chain: lastSuccessful -> apiPrimary -> first available.

evidence.notes: Theme cycles through white->light->dark. Toasts auto-remove via setTimeout. Three themes: white (default), light, dark.

test_descriptions: defaults new installs to the pure white theme
hydrates and applies the pure white theme as a light color scheme
cycles through pure white, warm classic, and dark themes

evidence.notes: Tauri-only update flow. Startup check delayed 5s. Supports proxy from settingsStore. Install flow: download -> prepare_for_update_install -> install -> relaunch. Error recovery calls cancel_update_install.

test_descriptions: stores available update metadata after a successful check
passes the configured manual update proxy to update checks
does not re-prompt for the same version after dismissing once
prompts again when a newer version is available after dismissing an older one
downloads, stops sidecars, installs, and relaunches
refreshes the pending update when the proxy changes before install
clears the native exit guard when install fails after sidecars stop

evidence.notes: Manages workspace references attached to chat sessions. Reference kinds: file, code-comment, code-selection, chat-selection. Deduplicates by kind+path+line+note. formatWorkspaceReferencePrompt generates markdown prompt for LLM context injection.

test_descriptions: deduplicates file references per session
formats line comments into the request prompt
formats selected code without requiring a comment
formats selected chat messages as chat context instead of file context
does not add prompt text for plain file attachments

evidence.notes: Complex per-session workspace panel management. Supports file tree exploration, file/diff preview tabs with close scopes (current/others/left/right/all). Uses request ID tracking to handle stale async responses. Preview kinds: file and diff. View modes: changed and all.

test_descriptions: uses hoisted session api mocks
keeps panel open state and active view isolated per session
loads workspace status successfully
defaults an empty changed-files status to the all-files view
keeps the changed-files view when status contains changes
returns to changed-files when a refreshed default all-files view now has changes
does not override an explicit all-files selection when refreshed status has changes
does not override an explicit changed-files selection when status is empty
captures workspace status request errors
lazily loads tree nodes when expanding and reuses cached results
ignores stale tree responses for the same session path
opens preview tabs, supports multiple kinds, and reuses duplicates without persistence
closes exact tab id and preserves sibling preview for the same path
chooses the next sensible active preview when closing the current tab
closes preview tabs by context-menu scope
ignores stale status responses for the same session
ignores stale preview responses after close and reopen
does not recreate loading or error state when a loading preview is closed before completion
clears session UI and cached data for clearSession and resetSessionUi

sections: self-hosted fonts (Inter, Manrope, JetBrains Mono, Material Symbols Outlined)
Semantic CSS variable aliases for light/white/dark themes
Keyframe animations (shimmer, spin, pulse-dot, progress-fill, composer-drop)
evidence.design_tokens: Comprehensive Material Design 3 inspired token system with surface, primary, secondary, tertiary, error, success, info, warning colors. Includes code/diff syntax highlighting tokens, terminal tokens, activity heatmap tokens, inspector tokens, sidebar tokens.
evidence.notes: Three complete theme definitions. Self-hosted fonts avoid Google CDN. Tailwind v4 @theme block for design tokens. Reduced-motion media queries throughout. Dark theme uses warm accent (#FFB59F). White theme is neutral/blue-gray.

test_descriptions: defines activity and status tokens for every supported theme
avoids color-mix in the startup-critical UI zoom shell chrome for Safari 15 WebView support
keeps the UI zoom slider thumb visible in dark mode

evidence.notes: Configuration types for messaging platform adapters: Telegram, Feishu (Lark), WeChat, DingTalk. Each adapter has platform-specific fields (bot tokens, app IDs, allowed users, paired users). Supports pairing state with expiry.

Central type file for the entire chat/message system. Defines WebSocket protocol between client and server, UI message rendering model, computer use permission system, background agent tasks, goal events, memory events.
CLI V2 task types. CLITask has blocking relationships (blocks/blockedBy arrays). TaskListSummary provides aggregate counts by status.

MCP (Model Context Protocol) server configuration types. Supports stdio, http, and sse transports. McpServerRecord tracks connection status (connected, needs-auth, failed, disabled, checking) and editability flags.
Memory system types for persistent project knowledge. MemoryProject tracks existence, file count, and current project flag. MemoryFile has metadata including type, description, and isIndex flag.

Plugin system types. Scopes: user, project, local, managed, builtin. Capabilities: commands, agents, skills, hooks, mcpServers, lspServers. PluginSummary/Detail for listing and detail views. Marketplace support with auto-update. Session reload tracks applied changes.

AI provider configuration types. ApiFormat: anthropic, openai_chat, openai_responses. Auth strategies: api_key, auth_token, auth_token_empty_api_key, dual_same_token, dual_dummy. ModelMapping has main/haiku/sonnet/opus slots. ProviderTestResult has connectivity and proxy steps.

Provider preset template types. ProviderPreset defines default configurations for AI providers including base URL, API format, default models, auth requirements, and website URLs. Featured flag for prominent display.
Module declaration for 'qrcode' package. Declares toDataURL method for generating QR code data URLs with options for error correction, margin, scale, width, and colors.
Simple type for runtime model selection: providerId (nullable) and modelId.

Largest type file (547 lines). Defines the entire workflow system: templates (draft/detail/import/export/validate), phases with skill references and artifact requirements, workflow session lifecycle (created->running->pending-confirmation->completed/failed/cancelled), phase transitions with handoff, linked workflow sessions with context strategies, and basic session/message types. Workflow skill resolution system tracks availability from multiple sources (user, project, plugin, managed, bundled, mcp).

Core settings types. THEME_MODES = ['white', 'light', 'dark']. PermissionMode: default, acceptEdits, plan, bypassPermissions, dontAsk. WebSearchMode: auto, anthropic, tavily, brave, disabled. AppMode: default or portable. H5AccessSettings for mobile web access. DesktopTerminalSettings for shell configuration.

Skill system types. SkillSource: user, project, plugin, mcp, bundled. SkillCatalogStatus: available, missing, plugin-disabled, unsupported-source. SkillMeta tracks provenance (sourcePath, namespace, pluginName, contentHash). SkillDetail includes file tree and file contents with frontmatter.

Scheduled task (cron) types. CronTask supports recurring/permanent flags, permission mode, model selection, worktree isolation, and notification channels (desktop, telegram, feishu). TaskRun tracks execution status and output.

Multi-agent team types. AgentColor: 8 color options. TeamMember tracks runtime (providerId, modelId), session, token usage, join time. AGENT_LIFECYCLE_TYPES filters internal messages (shutdown_approved, shutdown_rejected, etc.).
Vite client type reference declaration.
TypeScript config targeting ES2021 with bundler module resolution. Strict mode with noUnusedLocals, noUnusedParameters, noFallthroughCasesInSwitch, noUncheckedIndexedAccess. @ path alias to src/.

test_descriptions: keeps production bundles loadable in the macOS 12 Safari 15 WebView
does not rely on CSS color-mix for startup-critical shell chrome

evidence.summary: Architecture overview: Desktop Webapp Settings -> /api/adapters -> ~/.claude/adapters.json -> adapters/<platform>/index.ts -> /api/sessions + /ws/:sessionId -> Claude Code session. Four IM adapters (telegram, feishu, wechat, dingtalk) share a common layer. Manual bun run required per adapter. Bidirectional image/file support via AttachmentRef protocol.

Package name: claude-code-im-adapters. Dependencies: @larksuiteoapi/node-sdk (feishu), dingtalk-stream (dingtalk), grammy (telegram), ws (WebSocket bridge). DevDeps: bun-types, @types/ws. Scripts for each adapter and test suites.
Per-chatId serial queue using Promise chain. Prevents concurrent message processing for same chatId. Auto-cleans queue entry after completion to avoid memory leaks. 25 lines.

Adapter configuration loader. Priority: env vars > ~/.claude/adapters.json > defaults. Exports types: TelegramConfig(botToken, allowedUsers, pairedUsers, defaultWorkDir), FeishuConfig(appId, appSecret, encryptKey, verificationToken, streamingCard), WechatConfig(accountId, botToken, baseUrl, userId), DingtalkConfig(clientId, clientSecret, endpoint, permissionCardTemplateId). Unified AdapterConfig type with serverUrl, pairing state, per-platform configs.

IM message formatting utilities. Exports: splitMessage(text, limit) splits at paragraph/sentence/word boundaries; formatToolUse for tool display; formatPermissionRequest; truncateInput; escapeMarkdownV2; formatImHelp; formatImStatus(summary) renders session state with project/branch/model/status/tasks. AdapterChatState enum: idle/thinking/streaming/tool_executing/permission_pending.

AdapterHttpClient wraps REST calls to Claude Code Desktop server. Methods: createSession(workDir), listRecentProjects(), matchProject(query) with fuzzy matching by index/name/path, getGitInfo(sessionId), getTasksForSession(sessionId). Converts ws:// to http:// base URL. Has path safety: isPathWithinAllowedRoots validation. 30s default timeout with AbortController.

MessageBuffer for streaming text. Accumulates content_delta, flushes by time window (500ms default) or character threshold (200 chars). Handles complete() for final flush with in-flight protection. Used by Telegram editMessage and DingTalk AI card updates.
MessageDedup prevents duplicate processing. Map + TTL (10min) + capacity (5000). Sweep every 60s. tryRecord(id) returns true for new, false for duplicate. Evicts oldest when at capacity.

User pairing system for IM adapters. generatePairingCode() creates 6-char code from safe alphabet (no 0/O/1/I/L). isPaired() checks allowedUsers + pairedUsers union. tryPair() validates code with rate limiting (5 attempts per 5min), writes to config file atomically. isAllowedUser() for runtime auth check. Codes expire after 60 minutes.

Permission command parsing for IM. parsePermissionCommand handles /allow, /always, /deny with requestId. Also supports shortcut numbers (1=allow, 2=always, 3=deny) and Chinese equivalents. parsePermitCallbackData for inline keyboard callbacks (Telegram). formatPermissionInstructions and formatPermissionDecisionStatus for display.

Persistent chatId->sessionId mapping stored in ~/.claude/adapter-sessions.json. SessionStore class with get/set/delete/deleteBySessionId/listAll. Atomic writes via tmp+rename. Handles EPERM/EEXIST/EBUSY/EXDEV race conditions by falling back to direct write.

WsBridge manages WebSocket connections to Claude Code Desktop server /ws/:sessionId. Features: per-chatId session management, auto-reconnect with exponential backoff (1s base, 30s max, 10 attempts), 30s heartbeat, per-chat handler serialization via handlerChains (prevents state races at await points). Exports: connectSession, sendUserMessage(with attachments), sendPermissionResponse, sendStopGeneration, onServerMessage, resetSession, waitForOpen, destroy. AttachmentRef type with file/image/data support.

Size/MIME restrictions for IM attachments. IMAGE_MAX_BYTES=10MB, FILE_MAX_BYTES=30MB. IMAGE_MIME_WHITELIST: jpeg/png/gif/webp. checkAttachmentLimit returns ok or {reason, hint} with Chinese error messages.

Local staging for IM-downloaded resources at ~/.claude/im-downloads/{platform}/{sessionId}/. AttachmentStore: resolvePath with collision avoidance (timestamp+random suffix), atomic write (tmp->rename), GC for files older than 24h (default) and .part orphans older than 10min.

Shared types: ImPlatform = feishu|telegram|wechat|dingtalk. LocalAttachment (kind/name/path/size/mimeType/buffer). PendingUpload (id/source with base64|path|url/alt). Re-exports AttachmentRef from ws-bridge.

ImageBlockWatcher detects markdown image references ![alt](src) in streaming assistant text. Supports base64, file://, http(s)://, and absolute paths. Deduplicates by fingerprint hash. Buffers partial markers across feed() calls. drain() returns all seen images. Used for outbound image upload to IM platforms.

DingTalk adapter main entry. Uses DWClient (dingtalk-stream) for bot messages via Stream protocol (no public webhook). TOPIC_ROBOT for messages, TOPIC_CARD for card callbacks. Supports: DM-only (conversationType=1), session webhook replies, AI card streaming (via DingTalkAiCardService), permission card via template, project picker, /new /help /status /clear /stop /projects commands. OAuth2 accessToken with caching. Session webhook per chatId for reply routing. Graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.

DingTalk message parsing utilities. DingTalkRobotMessage type with msgId, conversationType, senderStaffId, sessionWebhook, text/markdown/content. Functions: parseDingTalkPayload, isDingTalkDirectMessage (conversationType=1), getDingTalkSenderId, getDingTalkChatId (dm:senderId or group:conversationId), extractDingTalkText (text/markdown/richText), extractDingTalkAttachments (picture/file/richText with downloadCode).

DingTalkAiCardService for streaming AI card updates. Uses CardKit API: create card instance, deliver to target (user/group), stream content updates with rate limiting (20 QPS), finish with status update. Token auto-refresh before expiry. Rate limiter with token bucket + QPS backoff (2s). ensureTableBlankLines fixes markdown table rendering. Configurable timeout via CC_JIANGXIA_IM_CARD_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS env.

DingTalkMediaService downloads attachments via direct URL or downloadCode->URL resolution through /v1.0/robot/messageFiles/download API. Writes to AttachmentStore. Infers MIME from file extension. Supports image and file kinds.

DingTalk interactive permission card builder. buildDingTalkPermissionCardParams creates card template params with allow/always/deny buttons. parseDingTalkPermissionCardAction recursively parses card callback data through nested JSON/objects to extract requestId+allowed+rule. Supports multiple naming conventions for the same fields.

DingTalk streaming state management helpers. DingTalkStreamingState type holds per-chat aiCardBuffers, streamingCards, streamingCardText maps. resetDingTalkStreamingState clears all maps. finishAndResetDingTalkStreamingState completes buffer, runs finalize callback, then resets.

Feishu adapter main entry. Uses @larksuiteoapi/node-sdk Lark.Client + WSClient (WebSocket long connection, no public URL needed). EventDispatcher for im.message.receive_v1 and card.action.trigger. StreamingCard per chatId for CardKit streaming. ImageBlockWatcher for outbound image dispatch. Supports: p2p only (group ignored), Schema 2.0 interactive cards for project picker and permission requests with cross-directory warning, bot open_id resolution via /bot/v3/info. Commands: /new /help /status /clear /stop /projects. Auto-recovery from Invalid signature thinking errors.

Feishu CardKit API wrapper. 5-step flow: createCardEntity -> sendCardAsMessage -> streamCardContent -> setCardStreamingMode -> updateCardKitCard. Key constraints: monotonically increasing sequence numbers, full cumulative text (not delta) for streaming, must close streaming_mode before user interaction. CardKitApiError class for structured errors. withImCardRequestTimeout (15s default). STREAMING_ELEMENT_ID constant.

StreamingCard state machine for per-chat Feishu card lifecycle. Phases: idle->creating->streaming->finalizing->completed/aborted. Manages CardKit primary path + im.message.patch fallback. Throttled via FlushController. Supports: appendText, appendReasoning (rendered as blockquote preview), startTool/completeTool (inline tool trace). terminalText drops reasoning/tools for final card. Error recovery: 230020 skip frame, 230099 disable streaming, consecutive failures threshold (3) disable CardKit streaming. buildRenderedCard/buildErrorCard/buildInitialStreamingCard card builders.

Feishu inbound message parser. extractInboundPayload converts im.message.receive_v1 content+message_type into InboundPayload {text, pendingDownloads}. Supports: text, image (image_key), file/file_archive (file_key), post (rich text with text/md/img/file nodes).

FlushController: throttled + mutex-protected flush scheduler. Constants: CARDKIT_MS=100, PATCH_MS=1500, LONG_GAP_THRESHOLD_MS=2000, BATCH_AFTER_GAP_MS=300. Features: mutex prevents concurrent flushes, needsReflush for data during in-flight flush, cardMessageReady gate, long-gap batch mode (300ms delay after >2s idle). complete() locks out new flushes.

Feishu card markdown optimization. optimizeMarkdownForFeishu: H1-H3 heading downgrade (H2-H6->H5, H1->H4), code block protection, Schema 2.0
spacing for tables/headings/code, compress 3+ blank lines, strip non-img_* image refs. sanitizeTextForCard: limits tables to 3 (FEISHU_CARD_TABLE_LIMIT), wraps excess in code blocks to prevent 230099/11310 error. findMarkdownTablesOutsideCodeBlocks for table detection.

FeishuMediaService wraps Lark SDK media APIs. downloadResource: im.messageResource.get -> writeFile to AttachmentStore. uploadImage: im.image.create returns image_key. uploadFile: im.file.create with detectFeishuFileType (opus/mp4/pdf/doc/xls/ppt/stream). sendImageMessage/sendFileMessage. guessMime for image/file types.

Feishu CardKit error code parsing. CARD_ERROR: RATE_LIMITED=230020, CARD_CONTENT_FAILED=230099. CARD_CONTENT_SUB_ERROR: ELEMENT_LIMIT=11310. extractLarkApiCode handles SDK/Axios/nested structures. extractSubCode parses ErrCode from msg string. parseCardApiError returns {code, subCode, errMsg}. isCardRateLimitError and isCardTableLimitError predicates.

Cross-platform path safety check. isOutsideWorkDir(filePath, workDir) detects if file escapes project directory. Supports Windows (drive letter, UNC) and POSIX paths. Used in Feishu permission card to show cross-directory warning.

Telegram adapter using grammY Bot framework. Long-polling (bot.start()). Supports: private chat only, photo/document/video/audio/voice attachments, InlineKeyboard for permission requests (permit:requestId:yes/always/no), editMessageText for streaming with placeholder messages, ImageBlockWatcher for outbound images. Commands via bot.command: /start, /help, /new, /projects, /stop, /status, /clear, /allow, /always, /deny. Auto-recovery from Invalid signature thinking errors. TELEGRAM_TEXT_LIMIT=4000.

TelegramMediaService wraps grammY bot API. downloadFile: getFile -> fetch file_path URL -> write to AttachmentStore. classifyKind by MIME/extension. sendPhoto via InputFile(buffer). sendDocument via InputFile(buffer, fileName).

WeChat adapter using iLink QR protocol. Long-polling via getWechatUpdates (35s timeout). MessageBuffer for block-based streaming (3s interval). WechatTypingController for typing indicators with 5s keepalive. context_token management per chat. Supports: text and media (image/file via CDN download with AES-ECB decryption). Commands: /new /help /status /clear /stop /projects. No card/inline keyboard support. WECHAT_TEXT_LIMIT=3500.

WeChat iLink protocol implementation. QR login flow: startWechatLoginWithQr -> pollWechatLoginWithQr (wait/scaned/scaned_but_redirect/expired/confirmed). getWechatUpdates long-polling. sendWechatText with context_token. getWechatConfig for typing_ticket. sendWechatTyping. extractWechatText from item_list (text+voice+ref_msg). buildClientVersion from semver. API headers: iLink-App-Id, iLink-App-ClientVersion, AuthorizationType, X-WECHAT-UIN.

WeChat media service. collectWechatMediaCandidates extracts image (type=2) and file (type=4) from item_list with CDN URL and AES key. WechatMediaService.downloadCandidate fetches encrypted bytes, decrypts AES-128-ECB if aesKey present, writes to AttachmentStore. Default CDN base: findermp.video.qq.com.
WechatTypingController manages typing indicator keepalive. start(chatId) sends typing immediately then every 5s. stop(chatId) clears interval and sends cancel. destroy() clears all.

macOS Computer Use helper (~700 lines). Python script providing JSON protocol for desktop automation. Uses pyautogui, mss (screenshot), Quartz (display enumeration, window list), AppKit (workspace, pasteboard, URL), pyobjc. Key mapping (macOS: cmd->command, alt->option). Commands: check_permissions (AXIsProcessTrusted), list_displays, screenshot, key, hold_key, type, click, drag, move_mouse, scroll, mouse_down/up, cursor_position, frontmost_app, list_installed_apps, list_running_apps, open_app, read/write/paste_clipboard. Screen capture with JPEG quality 75.

Windows Computer Use helper (~750 lines). Same JSON protocol as mac_helper.py. Uses pyautogui, mss, screeninfo, win32gui/win32api/win32process, psutil, pyperclip. Key mapping (Windows: cmd->win, alt->alt). Commands: check_permissions (always True on Windows), list_displays (screeninfo+ctypes DPI), screenshot, key, hold_key, type, click, drag, move_mouse, scroll, cursor_position, frontmost_app, list_installed_apps (registry+Start Menu), list_running_apps (psutil), open_app, read/write/paste_clipboard. UTF-8 stdout forced at startup.

Cross-platform tests for mac_helper and win_helper. TestKeyMap: validates KEY_MAP entries, platform-specific mappings (macOS cmd->command, Windows cmd->win), common keys present in both. TestJSONProtocol: both helpers same command set, expected commands exist (check_permissions, list_displays, screenshot, key, type, click, etc.), unknown command returns error. TestHelperOutputFormat: json_output/error_output functions exist, main entry point. TestWinHelperPermissions: always True. TestMacHelperPermissions: AXIsProcessTrusted, osascript clipboard. TestCrossPlatformFunctions.
macOS runtime dependencies: mss, Pillow, pyautogui, pyobjc-core, pyobjc-framework-Cocoa, pyobjc-framework-Quartz.
Windows runtime dependencies: mss, Pillow, pyautogui, pywin32, psutil, pyperclip, screeninfo.

evidence.description: Top-level wrapper providing FpsMetricsProvider, StatsProvider, and AppStateProvider to the component tree. Uses React Compiler runtime (_c cache). Props: getFpsMetrics, stats, initialState, children.

Main message list container. Manages virtual scrolling, message normalization, grouping (tool use, read/search), and performance-critical rendering. Uses LogoHeader memoized component. Feature-gated PROACTIVE/KAIROS imports.

Custom select component system. Barrel export of SelectMulti, Select, and OptionWithDescription type. Uses hooks for state management (use-select-state, use-select-input, use-select-navigation, use-multi-select-state).

State machine for feedback survey: closed/open/thanks/transcript_prompt/submitting/submitted. Uses analytics event logging. Sub-components: FeedbackSurveyView, TranscriptSharePrompt, FeedbackSurveyThanks.

Hybrid markdown renderer: tables via React components (MarkdownTable), other content via ANSI strings (formatToken). Uses LRU token cache (500 entries) for marked.lexer performance. Fast-path for plain text (no markdown syntax detection).
Model selection UI with effort level support. Uses CustomSelect/Select, AppState, keybindings, settings persistence. Supports fast mode toggle. Writes model + effort to user settings on selection.

Main logo/landing display. Shows recent activity, onboarding status, sandbox status, guest passes upsell, overage credit upsell, release notes. Feature-gated ChannelsNotice import. Uses AppState, global config, model settings.
Help dialog with tabs (Commands, General). Separates builtin vs custom commands. Uses keybindings, terminal size, modal context. Exports HelpV2 with onClose and commands props.

Multi-step onboarding wizard: preflight/theme/oauth/api-key/security/terminal-setup. Uses theme picker, OAuth flow, API key approval. Analytics event tracking per step.

Bundled skill registration system. BundledSkillDefinition type with: name, description, aliases, whenToUse, argumentHint, allowedTools, model, hooks, context, agent, files, getPromptForCommand. Lazy file extraction with symlink-safe O_NOFOLLOW|O_EXCL writes. Per-process nonce directory for security.

Bundled skills initializer. Registers 12+ skills at startup with feature-gating: updateConfig, keybindings, verify, debug, loremIpsum, skillify, remember, simplify, batch, stuck, dream (KAIROS), hunter (REVIEW_ARTIFACT), loop (AGENT_TRIGGERS), scheduleRemoteAgents (AGENT_TRIGGERS_REMOTE), claudeApi (BUILDING_CLAUDE_APPS), claudeInChrome, runSkillGenerator (RUN_SKILL_GENERATOR).
Debug skill: reads tail of session debug log (64KB), enables debug logging if off, provides diagnostic prompt. Uses Read/Grep/Glob tools. disableModelInvocation=true.

Batch/parallel work orchestration skill. Generates plan with 5-30 work units, spawns worker agents in isolated git worktrees. Uses AgentTool, PlanMode, AskUserQuestion, SkillTool (simplify). Two-phase: research+plan then spawn workers.

Memory review skill (ant-only). Reviews auto-memory landscape, classifies entries by destination (CLAUDE.md/CLAUDE.local.md/team memory/auto-memory), identifies duplicates/outdated/conflicts. Does NOT apply changes without approval.

Auto-generated stub for MCP skill resolution. Provides no-op Proxy for all imports. Exports stub functions for cached MC state, coordinator context, protected namespaces. Used as DCE placeholder for feature-gated modules.

Supports: frontmatter parsing, path-based conditional skills, dynamic skill discovery, deduplication via realpath, bare mode, plugin-only policy. Key functions: getSkillDirCommands, createSkillCommand, parseSkillFrontmatterFields, discoverSkillDirsForPaths, activateConditionalSkillsForPaths.
